message:
feat(agents): add config template generation for detected agents (ft-dr6zv.2.4)

Core library module for generating agent-specific config files (CLAUDE.md,
AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, CONVENTIONS.md, copilot-instructions.md) with
idempotent merge semantics, dry-run planning, and backup-before-write design.

- agent_config_templates.rs: 5 config kinds, template generation for all 12
  known agents, marker-fenced merge logic, build_generation_plan for dry-run
- robot_types.rs: AgentConfigureData, AgentConfigureDryRunData, plan/result
  item types for ft robot agents configure API surface
- 45 unit tests + 16 property tests, all passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.2.1","title":"Add franken-agent-detection workspace dependency with feature gate","description":"## Context
The `franken-agent-detection` crate (v0.1.0, published on crates.io) provides synchronous filesystem-based probes for 9 coding agents. It needs to be added to FrankenTerm's workspace.

## Scope
1. Add `franken-agent-detection = "0.1.0"` to workspace `[dependencies]` in root Cargo.toml
2. Add `franken-agent-detection` as optional dependency in frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml
3. Create `agent-detection` feature flag gating the dependency
4. Add `#[cfg(feature = "agent-detection")] pub mod agent_detection;` to lib.rs
5. Include in default features so it's always available
6. Verify clean build with and without the feature
7. Document feature flag in Cargo.toml with inline comments

## Key Considerations
- The crate has minimal dependencies (serde, thiserror, chrono, dirs) — no conflict risk
- It's synchronous (no async runtime needed) — can be called directly from any context
- The crate is already published on crates.io, so use versioned dependency (not git)
- Verify crate version compatibility: check if newer version available on crates.io

## Testing Requirements
- `test_feature_flag_on`: `cargo check --workspace` passes with agent-detection feature enabled
- `test_feature_flag_off`: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` passes cleanly
- `test_cfg_module_accessible`: `franken_agent_detection` types importable when feature enabled
- `test_cfg_module_absent`: feature off → module not compiled, no dead code warnings
- `test_no_dep_conflicts`: `cargo tree -d` shows no duplicate versions of serde/thiserror/chrono
- Run as CI script: `tests/ci/check_agent_detection_feature.sh`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check --workspace` passes with agent-detection feature
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` passes without it
- Feature flag documented in Cargo.toml
- CI script validates both builds

 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.2.2","title":"Unified agent detection module merging runtime + filesystem probes","description":"## Context
FrankenTerm currently detects agents at runtime via `agent_correlator.rs` (3-layer: pattern engine → pane title → process name). The `franken-agent-detection` crate adds a 4th layer: filesystem-based installation probing that detects agents whether or not they're running.

**Related prior work:**
- `ft-dug` (CLOSED): Smart Environment Detection epic — included basic agent presence detection as part of broader environment probing. This bead extends that with the dedicated `franken-agent-detection` crate for much deeper, more structured agent inventory.
- `ft-2l27x.10` (CLOSED): Agent session correlation & metadata capture — established `AgentMetadata` struct and agent type detection via pattern engine + pane title + process name. This bead builds on that by adding filesystem-based detection as a 4th layer.
- `ft-dug.4` (within ft-dug): Environment detection API — detected agents as one of many environment signals. This bead makes agent detection first-class with a dedicated module and richer data.

## Scope
Create `agent_detection.rs` module that unifies both approaches:

1. **Startup detection** (new, via franken-agent-detection):
   - Call `detect_installed_agents()` at FrankenTerm startup
   - Cache the `InstalledAgentDetectionReport` in a global `OnceLock`
   - Report includes: slug, detected bool, evidence strings, root_paths for each of 9 agents
   - Supported agents: claude, codex, gemini, cursor, cline, opencode, aider, windsurf, continue
   - Detection probes: config directories (~/.claude/, ~/.codex/), binary presence in PATH, version files

2. **Runtime correlation** (existing, enhanced):
   - When `agent_correlator` identifies an agent in a pane, cross-reference with installation data
   - Enrich `PaneAgentState` with: installed version, config_path, binary_path from detection report
   - If agent_correlator detects an agent NOT in the installation report, log a diagnostic warning ("agent running but not found on filesystem — possible containerized or remote agent")
   - If detection report shows an agent installed but correlator never sees it running, note as "installed but idle"

3. **Unified `AgentInventory` struct**:
   - `installed: Vec<InstalledAgentDetectionEntry>` — what's on the system
   - `running: HashMap<PaneId, PaneAgentState>` — what's active in which pane
   - `summary()` method returning counts and categorized agent list (installed-only, running, installed+running)
   - `agent_by_slug(slug: &str)` for quick lookup
   - Serde-serializable for IPC and robot mode output

4. **Event bus integration**:
   - Emit `AgentDetected { pane_id, agent_type, detection_method }` when correlator identifies agent
   - Emit `AgentExited { pane_id, agent_type, exit_code }` when agent process exits
   - Events carry detection method (filesystem, pane_title, process_name, pattern_engine) for diagnostics

## Testing Requirements
- `test_startup_detection_all_9`: fixture dirs for all 9 agents → all detected
- `test_startup_detection_none`: empty fixtures → empty report, no panics
- `test_startup_detection_partial`: 3 of 9 installed → exactly 3 detected
- `test_detection_evidence`: each detected agent includes evidence string
- `test_detection_caching_oncelock`: second call returns cached, no filesystem I/O
- `test_detection_under_50ms`: timed test with mock filesystem
- `test_correlation_enrichment`: mock pane + detection report → enriched PaneAgentState
- `test_unknown_agent_diagnostic`: correlator finds agent not in report → warning logged
- `test_installed_but_idle`: agent in report but never correlated → correct category
- `test_inventory_summary_counts`: verify installed/running/both counts
- `test_event_bus_detected`: agent starts → AgentDetected event with correct fields
- `test_event_bus_exited`: agent exits → AgentExited event with exit code
- `test_feature_flag_off`: cfg(not(feature = "agent-detection")) → correlator works standalone
- All tests use structured JSON logging with agent_slug, detection_method, timing

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 9 agent types detected at startup if their configs/binaries exist
- agent_correlator runtime detection enriched with installation metadata
- Unified inventory queryable via single method call
- Detection runs in < 50ms (pure filesystem probes)
- Graceful fallback if franken-agent-detection feature disabled
- Event bus emits agent lifecycle events with detection method provenance
- Prior work from ft-dug and ft-2l27x.10 preserved and extended (not duplicated)
- All unit tests pass with structured logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureCanyon","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-16T04:53:57.310610Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:46:00.671047Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T16:46:00.671033Z","close_reason":"Implemented unified startup+runtime agent detection inventory and lifecycle event integration; validated targeted suites and workspace check via rch, with only unrelated pre-existing workspace clippy/fmt failures remaining.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-detection","correlation","inventory"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":913,"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.2.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Prior art: ft-dug (Smart Environment Detection, CLOSED) included basic agent presence detection. ft-2l27x.10 (Agent session correlation, CLOSED) established AgentMetadata struct and 3-layer detection. ft-dug.4 (Environment detection API, CLOSED) detected agents as one of many env signals. This bead extends all of that with the dedicated franken-agent-detection crate for structured, filesystem-based probing across 9 agent types.","created_at":"2026-02-16T05:17:06Z"},{"id":914,"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.2.2","author":"AzureCanyon","text":"Progress slice complete for ft-dr6zv.2.2: implemented unified startup+runtime agent inventory and lifecycle events across agent_detection/agent_correlator/events/runtime/wire_protocol + proptest_agent_correlator update for new DetectionSource::Filesystem.\n\nValidation (all heavy commands offloaded via rch exec):\n- PASS: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-azurecanyon-ft-dr6zv-22-check cargo check --workspace --all-targets\n- PASS: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-azurecanyon-ft-dr6zv-22-check cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib agent_correlator::tests:: -- --nocapture (67 passed)\n- PASS: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-azurecanyon-ft-dr6zv-22-check cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib events::tests:: -- --nocapture (119 passed)\n- PASS: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-azurecanyon-ft-dr6zv-22-check cargo test -p frankenterm-core agent_detection::tests:: -- --nocapture (agent_detection suite 6 passed before long tail integration harness was intentionally stopped)\n- FAIL (pre-existing workspace baseline): rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-azurecanyon-ft-dr6zv-22-check cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails in unrelated crates: rangeset/uds/pty/blob-leases)\n- FAIL (pre-existing workspace baseline): rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (many unrelated formatting diffs across repo)\n\nAgent Mail: re-registered AzureCanyon, posted progress to thread swarm-sync-2026-02-16, and reserved touched paths for ft-dr6zv.2.2.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.2.3","title":"IPC and robot mode agent inventory API","description":"## Context
The unified agent inventory needs to be exposed externally so agents and users can query what's installed and running.

## Scope
1. IPC status API:
   - Add `agent_inventory` field to status payload containing:
     - `installed_agents`: list of detected agents with slugs, versions, config paths, detection evidence
     - `running_agents`: map of pane_id → agent type + state + detection method
     - `summary`: counts (installed, running, configured, installed_but_idle)
2. Robot mode commands:
   - `ft robot agents list` — show all installed agents with detection evidence
   - `ft robot agents running` — show agents currently active in panes with enriched metadata
   - `ft robot agents detect --refresh` — re-run filesystem detection (clears OnceLock cache, re-probes)
3. Output formats: JSON, TOON (via tru integration from ft-165vw)
4. Event bus integration: emit `AgentDetected` / `AgentExited` events when correlator state changes
5. Error handling: when agent-detection feature disabled, return structured "feature not available" response

## Testing Requirements
- `test_ipc_agent_inventory_present`: status payload includes agent_inventory field
- `test_ipc_agent_inventory_structure`: field has installed_agents, running_agents, summary sub-fields
- `test_robot_agents_list_json`: JSON output includes slug, version, config_path, evidence per agent
- `test_robot_agents_list_toon`: TOON output is valid and parseable
- `test_robot_agents_running_empty`: no agents running → empty map, not error
- `test_robot_agents_running_with_agents`: mock agents in panes → correct mapping
- `test_robot_agents_detect_refresh`: refresh clears cache and re-probes → updated results
- `test_event_bus_agent_detected`: correlator identifies agent → AgentDetected event fires with pane_id, agent_type, detection_method
- `test_event_bus_agent_exited`: agent exits pane → AgentExited event fires with exit_code
- `test_feature_disabled_graceful`: feature off → robot commands return "feature not available" not crash
- `test_ipc_feature_disabled`: feature off → status payload omits agent_inventory cleanly
- All tests use structured JSON logging with command, response_time_ms, agents_detected

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ft robot agents list` returns structured agent inventory
- IPC status includes agent_inventory when agent-detection feature enabled
- Event bus emits agent lifecycle events with detection method provenance
- All output formats work (JSON, TOON)
- Graceful degradation when feature disabled
- All tests pass with structured logging

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deliver IPC/robot inventory APIs with stable schemas for installed/running agents, summary metrics, and refresh workflows.
2. Ensure lifecycle events (detected/exited) are emitted with provenance fields that support auditing and automation.
3. Preserve graceful feature-disabled behavior and non-crashing empty-state responses across API surfaces.
4. Provide comprehensive validation: unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scripts for list/running/refresh commands, event emission, and format coverage (JSON/TOON).
5. Require structured logging/diagnostics for command context, response timings, detected-agent counts, and event payload integrity with reproducible artifacts.
6. Closure requires evidence package showing schema stability and downstream consumability.","notes":"This bead is a prerequisite for final agent-detection integration gate ft-dr6zv.2.5. -{"id":"ft-dr6zv.2.4","title":"Auto-generate agent configuration files for detected agents","description":"## Context
Once FrankenTerm knows which agents are installed, it can auto-generate configuration files to teach each agent about FrankenTerm's capabilities — creating a seamless integration loop where detected agents automatically learn how to use FrankenTerm's robot mode, MCP server, and pane management.

## Scope
1. For each detected agent, generate agent-specific config snippets:
   - **Claude Code**: generate/update SKILL.md with FrankenTerm robot mode commands, MCP tool descriptions
   - **Codex**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections with FrankenTerm integration patterns
   - **Gemini**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections
   - **Cursor**: generate .cursorrules with FrankenTerm integration patterns and robot mode reference
   - **Cline**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections
   - **OpenCode**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections
   - **Aider**: generate CONVENTIONS.md section with FrankenTerm workflow
   - **Windsurf**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections
   - **Continue**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections

2. Auto-configuration trigger:
   - On first startup after agent detected, offer to generate configs (interactive prompt)
   - Robot mode: `ft robot agents configure --agent claude --dry-run` (non-interactive)
   - `ft robot agents configure --all` to generate for all detected agents
   - Never overwrite existing configs — merge or append only
   - Backup existing file before any modification (`.bak` with timestamp)

3. Config templates (per agent type):
   - Include FrankenTerm robot mode command reference (all `ft robot` subcommands)
   - Include MCP server connection details (if ft MCP feature enabled)
   - Include pane management and search commands
   - Include agent detection self-reference (agent can query its own detection status)
   - Templates stored in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/agent_config_templates/`

4. Config placement rules:
   - Project-level configs: placed in current working directory (SKILL.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules)
   - Global configs: placed in agent's global config directory (~/.claude/, ~/.codex/, etc.)
   - Configurable via `--scope project|global` flag

## Testing Requirements
- `test_config_claude_skill_md`: generates valid SKILL.md with ft robot commands
- `test_config_codex_agents_md`: generates valid AGENTS.md section for Codex
- `test_config_cursor_rules`: generates valid .cursorrules with FrankenTerm patterns
- `test_config_aider_conventions`: generates valid CONVENTIONS.md section
- `test_config_all_9_agents`: verify config generation works for all 9 agent types
- `test_config_merge_append`: existing file → new content appended after existing, not overwriting
- `test_config_merge_idempotent`: run twice → no duplicate content added
- `test_config_backup_created`: before modification, `.bak` file created with original content
- `test_config_dry_run`: `--dry-run` outputs what would be generated, writes no files
- `test_config_dry_run_json`: `--dry-run --format json` returns structured preview
- `test_config_syntax_valid`: generated configs parse without error in each agent's format
- `test_config_project_scope`: `--scope project` places files in working directory
- `test_config_global_scope`: `--scope global` places files in agent's config directory
- `test_config_template_completeness`: each template includes robot mode ref, MCP details, pane commands
- `test_config_mcp_conditional`: MCP section only included when ft MCP feature is enabled
- All tests use structured JSON logging with agent_slug, config_type, scope, file_path

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config generation works for all 9 agent types
- Dry-run mode shows what would be generated without writing
- Existing agent configs are never overwritten (append/merge only)
- Backup created before any modification
- Generated configs are syntactically valid for each agent's format
- Robot mode command available for programmatic config management
- Merge is idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
- All tests pass with structured logging

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Generate correct agent-specific configuration outputs for all supported agent types with accurate FrankenTerm integration guidance.
2. Guarantee safe mutation behavior: dry-run support, backup creation, and merge/append semantics that never destructively overwrite existing user configs.
3. Support project/global placement scopes with deterministic path rules and clear operator feedback.
4. Provide comprehensive validation: unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scripts across all supported agent families and failure scenarios (permissions, malformed existing files, partial installs).
5. Require structured logging/diagnostics for generation decisions, file operations, backup paths, and validation outcomes with replayable artifacts.
6.  {"id":"ft-dr6zv.2.5","title":"Agent detection integration test suite — unit tests, e2e scripts, fixtures, structured logging","description":"## Context
The agent detection integration adds a new crate dependency, unifies filesystem-based detection with runtime correlation, exposes agent inventory via IPC/robot mode, and auto-generates agent configs. Each subtask has its own acceptance criteria, but a comprehensive integration test suite is needed to validate the full detection-to-config pipeline end-to-end.

## Scope

### 1. Unit Test Expansion (per-module, run in CI)

**agent_detection.rs tests (unified module):**
- `test_startup_detection_all_9_agents`: with mock filesystem fixtures for each agent type (claude, codex, gemini, cursor, cline, opencode, aider, windsurf, continue), verify all 9 are detected
- `test_startup_detection_none_installed`: empty home directory → empty installation report, no panic
- `test_startup_detection_partial`: only claude + codex installed → exactly those two detected
- `test_detection_evidence_strings`: each detected agent includes human-readable evidence (e.g., "Found ~/.claude/config.json")
- `test_detection_caching`: second call returns cached OnceLock result, not re-probing filesystem
- `test_detection_under_50ms`: detection completes in < 50ms on mock filesystem (no real I/O)
- `test_runtime_correlation_enrichment`: when agent_correlator detects "claude" in pane, cross-reference adds version/config_path from detection report
- `test_runtime_unknown_agent_diagnostic`: agent_correlator detects agent NOT in installation report → diagnostic log emitted
- `test_agent_inventory_installed_field`: AgentInventory.installed contains exactly the detected agents
- `test_agent_inventory_running_field`: AgentInventory.running maps pane_id → PaneAgentState with enrichment
- `test_agent_inventory_summary`: summary() returns correct counts for installed, running, configured
- `test_feature_flag_graceful_fallback`: when agent-detection feature disabled, existing correlator still works (no compile errors, no panics)

**IPC/robot mode tests:**
- `test_ipc_status_agent_inventory`: status payload includes `agent_inventory` field with installed/running/summary
- `test_robot_agents_list_json`: `ft robot agents list --format json` returns structured JSON with slug, version, config_path, evidence per agent
- `test_robot_agents_list_toon`: `ft robot agents list --format toon` returns valid TOON output
- `test_robot_agents_running`: `ft robot agents running` shows pane_id → agent type mapping
- `test_robot_agents_detect_refresh`: `ft robot agents detect --refresh` re-probes filesystem and updates cache
- `test_event_bus_agent_detected`: when agent correlator identifies agent in pane, `AgentDetected` event fires
- `test_event_bus_agent_exited`: when agent process exits pane, `AgentExited` event fires
- `test_ipc_feature_flag_off`: when feature disabled, status payload omits agent_inventory without error

**Autoconfig tests:**
- `test_config_generation_claude_skill_md`: generates valid SKILL.md with FrankenTerm robot commands
- `test_config_generation_codex_agents_md`: generates valid AGENTS.md section for Codex
- `test_config_generation_cursor_rules`: generates valid .cursorrules with FrankenTerm patterns
- `test_config_generation_aider_conventions`: generates valid CONVENTIONS.md section
- `test_config_merge_no_overwrite`: existing config file not overwritten — new content appended/merged
- `test_config_dry_run`: `--dry-run` shows what would be generated without writing any files
- `test_config_all_9_agents`: config generation works for all 9 agent types
- `test_config_syntax_validation`: generated configs are syntactically valid (parse without error)
- `test_config_backup_before_merge`: existing file backed up before merge operation
- `test_robot_agents_configure`: `ft robot agents configure --agent claude --dry-run` produces structured output

### 2. Integration Tests (cross-module, fixture-based)

**Fixture directory structure** (created in tempdir for each test):
```
test_home/
├── .claude/
│   └── config.json          # Claude Code detection
├── .codex/
│   └── config.yaml          # Codex detection
├── .cursor/
│   └── settings.json        # Cursor detection
├── .continue/
│   └── config.json          # Continue detection
└── bin/
    ├── claude               # Binary detection
    └── aider                # Binary detection
```

- `integration_detection_to_inventory`: run detection on fixture dirs → build AgentInventory → verify all fields correct
- `integration_detection_plus_correlation`: detection finds agents on disk + correlator finds agents in mock panes → unified inventory correct
- `integration_detection_to_ipc`: detection runs → IPC status includes agent_inventory → robot mode commands work
- `integration_detection_to_autoconfig`: detection finds agents → autoconfig generates valid configs for each
- `integration_autoconfig_idempotent`: run autoconfig twice → second run doesn't duplicate content
- `integration_event_lifecycle`: start agent in pane → AgentDetected fires → stop agent → AgentExited fires → inventory updated

### 3. E2E Test Scripts (shell scripts, run against live FrankenTerm)

**`tests/e2e/test_agent_detection.sh`:**
- Create mock agent installations in a test home directory
- Start FrankenTerm with HOME overridden to test directory
- Run `ft robot agents list --format json`
- Verify all mock-installed agents detected with correct evidence
- Verify agents NOT installed are NOT detected (no false positives)
- Log detection timing and results as structured JSON

**`tests/e2e/test_agent_lifecycle.sh`:**
- Start FrankenTerm with agent detection enabled
- Open a new pane and start a mock agent process (e.g., `echo "claude-code v2.0"`)
- Verify `ft robot agents running` shows the agent
- Kill the mock process
- Verify agent removed from running list
- Verify event bus logged AgentDetected and AgentExited events

**`tests/e2e/test_agent_autoconfig.sh`:**
- Create mock agent installations
- Run `ft robot agents configure --dry-run --format json`
- Verify output lists configs that would be generated for each agent
- Run `ft robot agents configure --agent claude`
- Verify SKILL.md created with FrankenTerm commands
- Run again — verify no duplicate content added
- Verify backup created of original file

**`tests/e2e/test_agent_detection_graceful.sh`:**
- Start FrankenTerm with `--no-default-features` (agent-detection off)
- Verify `ft robot agents list` returns graceful "feature not enabled" message
- Verify IPC status works without agent_inventory field
- Verify no crashes or error logs

### 4. Structured Logging Requirements

All test output MUST use structured JSON logging with these fields:
- `test_name`: the test function/script name
- `phase`: "setup" | "detect" | "correlate" | "assert" | "teardown"
- `timestamp_ms`: millisecond-precision timestamp
- `duration_ms`: time taken for the current phase
- `result`: "pass" | "fail" | "skip" | "error"
- `detail`: human-readable explanation
- `agents_detected`: list of agent slugs detected (when relevant)
- `metrics`: optional object with numeric measurements

Example log line:
```json
{"test_name":"test_startup_detection_all_9_agents","phase":"detect","timestamp_ms":1708070400000,"duration_ms":23,"result":"pass","detail":"Detected 9/9 agents from fixtures","agents_detected":["claude","codex","gemini","cursor","cline","opencode","aider","windsurf","continue"],"metrics":{"detection_time_ms":23,"agents_found":9}}
```

### 5. Fixture Management

Create a `tests/fixtures/agent_detection/` directory containing:
- `full_install/`: all 9 agents installed with realistic config files
- `partial_install/`: only claude + codex + cursor installed
- `empty_install/`: no agents installed
- `corrupt_install/`: malformed config files (test graceful handling)
- `version_variants/`: different versions of agent configs (test version parsing)
- Each fixture includes a `manifest.json` listing expected detection results for validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- All unit tests pass in CI (cargo test --workspace)
- Integration tests pass with agent-detection feature on and off
- E2E scripts exit 0 on a clean FrankenTerm build
- Fixture-based detection achieves 100% accuracy (no false positives, no false negatives)
- Structured logging captures every detection phase with timing
- Autoconfig idempotency verified (run twice, same result)
- Event bus lifecycle tests verify correct ordering (detected before exited)
- Test coverage for agent_detection module > 85% (measured by cargo-tarpaulin)
- All 9 agent types have fixture coverage

 {"id":"ft-dug","title":"[EPIC] Smart Environment Detection: auto-configure based on detected setup","description":"# [EPIC] Smart Environment Detection

## Mission
Automatically detect the user's environment and configure wa optimally, reducing setup friction.

## Why This Matters
Users struggle with configuration:
- "Which settings do I need?"
- "Why isn't it detecting my agents?"
- "What's the right poll interval?"

Smart detection provides:
- **Zero-config start**: Works out of the box
- **Optimal defaults**: Tuned for detected environment
- **Guided setup**: Suggests improvements

## Scope

### Environment Detection
- WezTerm version and capabilities
- Shell type (bash, zsh, fish)
- OSC 133 support
- Agent presence and types
- Remote panes (SSH)
- System resources (CPU, RAM)

### Auto-configuration
- Poll intervals based on system load
- Pattern packs based on detected agents
- Storage paths based on OS conventions
- Safety defaults based on environment

### Detection API
```rust
pub struct DetectedEnvironment {
    wezterm: WeztermInfo,
    shell: ShellInfo,
    osc_133_support: bool,
    agents: Vec<DetectedAgent>,
    remotes: Vec<RemoteHost>,
    system: SystemInfo,
}
```

### CLI Integration
- `wa doctor` shows detection results
- `wa setup` uses detection for suggestions
- Config shows effective settings with sources

## Success Criteria
- New users need no configuration to start
- Detection is accurate for common setups
- Suggestions improve user experience

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Environment detection API complete
- [ ] Auto-configuration uses detection
- [ ] wa doctor shows detection results
- [ ] Suggestions are actionable
- [ ] Tests cover detection scenarios

## Testing
- Unit tests for probes and recommendation rules.
- Integration tests for doctor/setup output.
- E2E artifacts include detection results and rationale.
